summary: Descriptive overview of rico in substantive law: pattern of racketeering, enterprise element, treble damages.
key_points: pattern of racketeering is a fundamental concept in criminal law.
Legal standards for rico are established through case law and statutory interpretation.
Courts apply specific tests to determine rico in litigation.
The doctrine of rico has evolved through Supreme Court precedents.
Understanding rico requires analysis of both historical context and modern applications.
philosophy_guard_statement: This fragment describes existing legal doctrines and their applications without advocating for normative changes.
